name: Shirodhara
body: A continuous medicated oil stream poured over the Ajna chakra — proven to reduce cortisol and dissolve chronic anxiety and insomnia.
long_description: Shirodhara is one of the most profoundly calming therapies in classical Ayurveda. A warm, steady stream of medicated oil — precisely selected for your constitution — is poured continuously over the Ajna (third eye) chakra and forehead in a rhythmic, pendulum motion. This deeply sensory experience activates the hypothalamus, lowers cortisol levels, and induces a state of meditative calm. At Ayursiha, the session is preceded by a brief Abhyanga (oil massage) of the head and shoulders to prepare the tissues and maximise absorption.
benefits: Clinically proven reduction in cortisol and stress hormones
Relief from chronic anxiety, depression, and burnout
Improvement in insomnia and sleep quality
Alleviation of headaches and migraines
Enhanced concentration, memory, and sensory acuity
Deeply calming effect on the nervous system
ideal: Those experiencing stress, anxiety, insomnia, burnout, or neurological complaints.
diet_plan: Favour Vata-pacifying foods: warm, oily, and grounding in nature
Include warm milk with ashwagandha or nutmeg before bedtime
Avoid stimulants: caffeine, alcohol, and refined sugars
Eat at regular intervals — do not skip meals or eat late at night
Light, easily digestible dinners such as soups and soft grains

name: Ayurvedic Consultation
body: Comprehensive Prakriti and Vikriti analysis with a senior physician. Pulse diagnosis, lifestyle review, and a personalised treatment roadmap.
long_description: Our Ayurvedic Consultation is the cornerstone of your healing journey at Ayursiha. Conducted by a senior Ayurvedic physician with decades of clinical experience, this session begins with Nadi Pareeksha — the ancient art of pulse diagnosis — to assess your unique constitution (Prakriti) and current state of imbalance (Vikriti). Combined with a thorough review of your lifestyle, diet, sleep patterns, and medical history, our physician creates a precise, personalised treatment roadmap tailored to restore your natural equilibrium.
benefits: In-depth Prakriti and Vikriti assessment
Nadi Pareeksha (pulse diagnosis) by an experienced physician
Personalised diet and lifestyle recommendations
Customised herbal and Rasayana prescriptions
A clear treatment roadmap for your healing journey
ideal: First-time visitors, those seeking a personalised wellness plan, or anyone managing chronic health conditions.
diet_plan: Favour warm, freshly cooked meals — avoid cold, processed, and leftover foods
Include easily digestible grains: rice, moong dal, and wheat
Incorporate digestive spices: ginger, cumin, coriander, and turmeric
Avoid heavy meats, fried foods, and excessive dairy
Eat at consistent times to stabilise Agni (digestive fire)

name: Abhyanga
body: Synchronised four-hand herbal oil massage. Nourishes all seven dhatus, stimulates lymphatic flow, and calms Vata.
long_description: Abhyanga is the classical Ayurvedic full-body oil massage, performed at Ayursiha in the traditional four-hand (Dwi Hasta) style by two trained therapists working in perfect synchrony. Using warm herbal oils formulated for your dosha, this therapy nourishes the seven dhatus (body tissues) from the skin inward, stimulates lymphatic circulation, and pacifies Vata — the primary dosha responsible for dryness, anxiety, and degeneration. The rhythmic pressure and herbal actives penetrate deep into the musculature, loosening adhesions and preparing the body for further detoxification.
benefits: Nourishment of all seven dhatus (body tissues)
Stimulation of lymphatic flow and immune function
Relief from muscle stiffness, joint pain, and fatigue
Calming of Vata dosha — reduces anxiety and nervous tension
Improvement in skin texture and lustre
Prepares the body for deeper Panchakarma procedures
ideal: Anyone seeking relaxation, relief from muscle and joint discomfort, or as a preparatory therapy for Panchakarma.
diet_plan: Light meals 2 hours before and after therapy — avoid a full stomach
Include nourishing, Vata-pacifying foods: sesame, warm soups, ghee, and root vegetables
Avoid dry, cold, and raw foods which aggravate Vata dosha
Stay well-hydrated with warm water or herbal broths throughout the day
Favour sweet, sour, and salty tastes to balance the effects of the therapy

name: Kizhi Therapy
body: Warm bolus massage using medicated rice, herbal powders, or leaves — highly effective for musculoskeletal conditions and joint inflammation.
long_description: Kizhi (meaning "bundle" in Malayalam) is a Kerala-specific therapeutic technique in which warm boluses — tightly packed with medicated rice (Njavara Kizhi), herbal powders (Podikizhi), or medicinal leaves (Elakizhi) — are rhythmically applied to the body. The combination of heat, pressure, and herbal actives penetrates deeply into the joints and muscles, making this treatment exceptionally effective for inflammatory and degenerative musculoskeletal conditions. At Ayursiha, the choice of Kizhi formulation is individually prescribed based on your diagnosis and constitution.
benefits: Reduction of joint inflammation, stiffness, and swelling
Relief from arthritis, spondylosis, and sports injuries
Improvement in muscle tone and flexibility
Enhanced circulation to peripheral tissues
Nourishment of the skin through herbal absorption
Relief from sciatica and lower back conditions
ideal: Those with arthritis, joint pain, sports injuries, spondylosis, or chronic musculoskeletal conditions.
diet_plan: Anti-inflammatory diet: emphasise turmeric, ginger, and bitter greens
Avoid curd, cold foods, and heavy non-vegetarian meals that increase Ama
Include warm soups, lightly spiced lentils, and cooked vegetables
Reduce Kapha-aggravating foods: dairy, sweets, and wheat
Warm water with lemon and ginger first thing each morning

name: Panchakarma
body: Five classical purification procedures administered in sequence to achieve deep cellular detoxification and metabolic reset.
long_description: Panchakarma — meaning "five actions" — is Ayurveda's most powerful therapeutic protocol. At Ayursiha, this multi-day programme is administered under continuous physician supervision, using classical formulations prepared in our in-house pharmacy. The five core procedures — Vamana, Virechana, Basti, Nasya, and Raktamokshana — are sequenced after a preparatory phase (Poorvakarma) of Snehana (oleation) and Swedana (sudation) therapies. The result is a profound cellular detoxification, metabolic reset, and restoration of doshic equilibrium.
benefits: Deep cellular detoxification and metabolic reset
Elimination of accumulated Ama (toxins)
Restoration of doshic balance and digestive fire (Agni)
Relief from chronic conditions including arthritis, insomnia, and skin disorders
Enhanced mental clarity and emotional wellbeing
Post-treatment Rasayana for sustained rejuvenation
ideal: Those seeking deep detoxification, management of chronic diseases, or a complete health reset.
diet_plan: Follow a strict Kitchari diet (rice and moong dal) throughout the programme
All meals freshly prepared with medicated ghee as prescribed by your physician
Avoid raw foods, heavy proteins, alcohol, and caffeine entirely
Warm water or herbal teas only — no cold beverages
Meal timings strictly follow physician-prescribed intervals to support purification

name: Rasayana Programme
body: Post-treatment rejuvenation combining classical formulations and dietary protocols. Rebuilds ojas — the marker of deep vitality.
long_description: Rasayana — the science of rejuvenation — is the eighth and most celebrated branch of classical Ayurveda. The Rasayana Programme at Ayursiha is designed as a post-treatment phase to consolidate the gains of detoxification and build deep, lasting vitality. Drawing on classical texts including the Charaka Samhita, our physicians prescribe individualised Rasayana formulations — adaptogenic herbs such as Ashwagandha, Amalaki, Shatavari, and Brahmi — alongside precise dietary protocols and lifestyle guidelines. The goal is to rebuild Ojas: the refined essence of all seven dhatus and the ultimate marker of immunity, radiance, and mental resilience.
benefits: Rebuilding of Ojas — the essence of deep vitality and immunity
Anti-ageing effects at the cellular and tissue level
Enhanced mental clarity, memory, and emotional stability
Sustained energy without stimulants
Strengthened immune response
Long-term maintenance of doshic balance post-Panchakarma
ideal: Post-Panchakarma patients, those recovering from illness, or anyone seeking long-term rejuvenation and anti-ageing benefits.
diet_plan: Emphasise Rasayana foods: Amalaki (amla), ghee, honey, milk, and fresh fruits
Avoid incompatible food combinations (Viruddha Ahara) that diminish Ojas
Eat in a calm, distraction-free environment — mindful eating is part of the therapy
Seasonal and locally sourced produce preferred for maximum prana
Physician-prescribed herbal supplements to be taken at specific meal intervals
